title: Brainstorming Session for a New Product Idea

all.en: Our team gathered in the bright meeting room, armed with sticky notes and markers.
all.zh: 我們團隊聚集在明亮的會議室，手裡拿著便利貼和麥克筆。

all.en: The team leader wrote a single question on the whiteboard: what do customers really want?
all.zh: 組長在白板上寫下一個簡單的問題：顧客真正想要的是什麼？

all.en: We agreed there would be no judgment, no matter how strange an idea sounded.
all.zh: 我們約定不論點子聽起來多奇怪，都不互相批評。

all.en: Someone suggested a smart umbrella that warns you of rain before you leave home.
all.zh: 有人提議做一款智慧雨傘，能在你出門前提醒你天氣會下雨。

all.en: Another teammate proposed a subscription service for fresh, ready-to-cook meal kits.
all.zh: 另一位同事則建議推出一項提供新鮮、現成可煮料理包的訂閱服務。

all.en: We laughed at some wild ideas, but they often sparked more practical ones.
all.zh: 我們對一些天馬行空的點子哈哈大笑，但這些常常激發出更實用的想法。

all.en: The whiteboard quickly filled with colorful notes covering every inch of space.
all.zh: 白板很快被五顏六色的便利貼貼滿，沒有一寸空間留下。

all.en: We grouped similar ideas together and identified three promising directions.
all.zh: 我們把相似的點子歸類在一起，找出三個有發展潛力的方向。

all.en: The designer sketched rough concepts while the marketer estimated potential demand.
all.zh: 設計師畫出粗略的概念圖，行銷人員則估算潛在的市場需求。

all.en: After two hours, we picked one idea to develop further next week.
all.zh: 兩個小時後，我們挑出一個點子，準備下週進一步發展。

all.en: I left the room feeling energized, as if anything were possible.
all.zh: 離開會議室時，我充滿幹勁，彷彿一切皆有可能。
